assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met (see criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md for detailed reasoning) |'. Lines 19-23 detail each criterion as PASS: single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, unchanged response shape. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) ends with 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: '| Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files in the diff match the task specification exactly. Modified: `modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs`, `modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs`. Created: `tests/api/package.rs`. No out-of-scope files. |'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on PR |'. report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created |'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are report/analysis files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). None contain evidence of code modification. The report describes reading the diff, analyzing code, and producing verdicts. No output mentions editing, writing, or modifying any source file.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with '### Overall: PASS' on line 25. No mention of merging, approving, or any automated merge action exists in any output file. The report presents a structured table of findings (lines 4-15) and detailed acceptance criteria analysis (lines 17-23) for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | Scanned all added lines. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, secrets, private keys, or credentials found. |'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` created with 4 tests. No existing tests were removed or modified. |'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected rows: Intent Alignment domain — Scope Containment (PASS, line 7), Diff Size (PASS, line 8), Commit Traceability (N/A, line 9); Security domain — Sensitive Patterns (PASS, line 10); Correctness domain — CI Status (PASS, line 11), Acceptance Criteria (PASS, line 12), Verification Commands (N/A, line 15); Style/Conventions domain — Test Quality (PASS, line 13, explicitly includes 'Eval Quality: N/A' and addresses repetitive test detection with 'avoid repetition' and test documentation with 'well-structured with Given/When/Then comments'), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE, line 14).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: Scope Containment row lists all 3 files individually with their categories (Modified vs Created) — a file-by-file comparison. Security: Sensitive Patterns row specifies what was scanned ('all added lines') and what patterns were checked ('hardcoded passwords, API keys, secrets, private keys, or credentials'). Correctness: Acceptance Criteria Detail section (lines 17-23) provides per-criterion code-level verification citing specific functions (validate_license_param, Expression::parse), code constructs (is_in, InnerJoin), return types, and test assertions. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain 'What was checked' and 'Evidence' sections with file paths, line numbers, and code references. Style/Conventions: Test Quality row describes test structure ('Given/When/Then comments'), data strategy ('distinct seed data'), coverage ('4 integration tests'), and individual test purposes.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13, Test Quality row ends with 'Eval Quality: N/A'. The Test Quality verdict is PASS based on the other sub-components (repetitive test detection, test documentation) without Eval Quality affecting the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13: 'Eval Quality: N/A'). No output file mentions creating eval failure sub-tasks. Root-Cause Investigation is N/A with 'No sub-tasks created' (report.md line 6), confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created |'. These appear as the first two rows in the table (lines 5-6), before domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), with self-contained reasons independent of domain analysis ('No review comments on PR' and 'No sub-tasks created').
